req.title: External ACH transfer (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines an ACH transfer endpoint with a $10,000 per-transfer limit, step-up verification for amounts above that limit, and specific error codes for insufficient funds (409) and exceeded limits without step-up (422). Test partitions are: (1) valid transfer within limit — EP positive, (2) transfer at exact boundary $10,000 — BVA boundary, (3) transfer $1 above limit without step-up — BVA/NEG boundary negative, (4) insufficient funds — NEG/DT negative, (5) unauthorized caller — NEG negative, (6) state transition from pending to queued — STC positive. This covers all mandatory error pathways and positive outcomes cited in R-FIN-054.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit
preconditions: Customer account exists and is active
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000 and verified ACH account id
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-054: transfers up to $10,000 shall be accepted
R-FIN-054: ACH batch queued; confirmation sent
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_within_limit(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_boundary
preconditions: Customer account exists and is active
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000 (exactly at limit) without step-up token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-054: up to $10,000 per transfer — boundary is inclusive
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_boundary(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):

        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],

        "currency": "USD"

    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_stepup_returns_422
preconditions: Customer account exists and is active
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 and no step-up header
Capture HTTP response status and error body
trace: R-FIN-054: transfers above $10,000 require step-up verification
R-FIN-054: error pathway 422 when amount > 10000 without step-up
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_without_stepup_returns_422(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 10000.01,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer account exists and is active
Account balance is $100 (insufficient for requested transfer)
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000 where balance < amount
Capture HTTP response status and error body
trace: R-FIN-054: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, verified_ach_account, low_balance_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": low_balance_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_unauthorized_caller_returns_401
preconditions: No valid auth token (expired or missing)
ACH link may or may not be verified
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=1000 and no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains error indicating authentication failure
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_unauthorized_caller_returns_401(unauthenticated_client, verified_ach_account):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": "<any_account_id>",
        "destination_ach_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 1000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = unauthenticated_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_stepup_transitions_to_queued
preconditions: Customer account exists and is active
Step-up verification token obtained via MFA challenge
actions: Complete MFA challenge to obtain step-up token
POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000 and X-Step-Up-Token header set
Capture HTTP response status and body
Verify transfer state transitions from 'pending' to 'queued'
expected: State transition: unauthenticated -> step-up-verified -> transfer queued
trace: R-FIN-054: step-up verification allows transfers above $10,000
R-FIN-054: ACH batch queued; confirmation sent
R-FIN-054: state transition from step-up verified to queued
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_stepup_transitions_to_queued(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_account, stepup_token):
    payload = {
        "source_account_id": funded_account["id"],
        "destination_ach_id": verified_ach_account["id"],
        "amount": 15000,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    headers = {"X-Step-Up-Token": stepup_token}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_decision_table_all_conditions_matrix
preconditions: Multiple customer account states prepared: verified ACH + sufficient funds, verified ACH + insufficient funds, unverified ACH + sufficient funds
actions: POST /transfers/ach for each condition combination in decision table
Record status codes and error_codes for each call
expected: verified ACH + sufficient funds + amount<=10000 -> 202 queued
verified ACH + insufficient funds + amount<=10000 -> 409 INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
unverified ACH + sufficient funds + amount<=10000 -> 422 ACH_LINK_NOT_VERIFIED
trace: R-FIN-054: precondition ach_link verified must hold
R-FIN-054: error pathway 409 on insufficient funds
R-FIN-054: negative paths must be tested
